title: perf(art): back art-job stores with SQLite (drop full-file parse+rewrite)
intent: Finishes item 5 of PERFORMANCE_AUDIT_2026-07-04.md. The two file-based art-job stores parsed and rewrote the entire ~730KB data/art_jobs.json on every create/read (measured 22ms/parse, unbounded append-only growth) and accidentally shared one file with two incompatible record shapes. Each store now uses its own dedicated SQLite table with a one-time shape-filtered migration; public signatures are unchanged so no router call site is touched.

diff_articulation.what_changed: art_job_store.py (singular: id/lane/payload) now reads/writes a dedicated art_studio_jobs SQLite table via get_rmos_db().execute_query/execute_update, with a one-time migration that imports only lane/payload-shaped rows from the legacy JSON; create is an upsert (delete-then-insert) preserving the old append-and-latest-wins semantics. art_jobs_store.py (plural: id/job_type/rings/...) now delegates to the existing SQLiteArtJobsStore (art_jobs table), migrating only job_type-shaped legacy rows once when the table is empty. Both keep their ArtJob dataclasses and function signatures (create_art_job / get_job / get_art_job / _load / _load_jobs), so rosette_jobs_routes, root_art_router, rosette_jobs_router, rmos rosette_cam_router, and pipeline_ops_rosette are unchanged. The legacy data/art_jobs.json is retained as the migration source.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: Removes a measured per-request full-file parse+rewrite on live rosette job create/read paths and untangles two record shapes that shared one file (the plural typed reader crashed on singular rows and vice versa). Not cosmetic: it changes the persistence backend while preserving the public contract, with a shape-filtered one-time data migration and new tests.

commands_run: pytest tests/test_art_job_stores_migration.py -q -> 5 passed (round-trip, upsert-no-dup, shape-filtered migration, decoupling, migrate-once)
manual: create/get/_load round-trips for both stores against a temp rmos.db + temp legacy JSON, decoupling confirmed

files.intent: Singular art-job store: SQLite-backed art_studio_jobs table + one-time JSON migration; upsert create.
files.behavior_change: Reads/writes SQLite instead of rewriting the whole JSON file; one-time migration of lane/payload rows; create is now an upsert. Public signatures unchanged.

files.intent: Plural art-job store: delegate to SQLiteArtJobsStore + one-time JSON migration of job_type-shaped rows.
files.behavior_change: Reads/writes SQLite via SQLiteArtJobsStore; created_at now an ISO string (was float epoch); public signatures unchanged.

files.intent: Cover round-trip, upsert, shape-filtered migration, decoupling, and migrate-once.

architecture_scan.acknowledgement_notes: Storage-backend change with preserved public contract and a one-time, shape-filtered, idempotent data migration; legacy JSON retained as source. Verified by 108 existing + 5 new tests.
